# Runner cutover: DELETE escalate from the data-integrity runner ‚Üí autonomous applyHeal (interim actuator + orchestrator wiring)

## Context ‚Äî child of #14132

#14132 (operator directive: DELETE `escalate`, 100% autonomous self-heal, no runtime operator) requires the data-integrity RUNNER path to stop routing to `escalateDiagnosis` and route every diagnosis to an autonomous heal terminal. The original runner (#14109, CLOSED) built the detect-producer scheduling **with** the escalate terminal; this leaf is the **integration cutover** that re-shapes that terminal to autonomous self-heal.

This is the runner-path slice of #14132. The actual heal OPERATIONS, the producer raw-evidence re-route, and the ADR amendments are sibling children (see Out of scope) ‚Äî this leaf makes escalate *structurally dead* in the runner and routes everything to the autonomous actuator.

## Scope ‚Äî fully delivered by the PR

- **Mode-classifier** (`dataIntegrityModeClassifier`) ‚Äî corruption-mode taxonomy ‚Üí autonomous terminal actions; single source for mode‚Üíaction. Zero escalate.
- **Runner re-shape** (`DataIntegrityDiagnosisService`) ‚Äî `escalateDiagnosis()` DELETED; `gatherAndDiagnose()` ‚Üí evidence-gatherer ‚Üí classify ‚Üí `applyHeals()` ‚Üí `recoveryActuator.applyHeal`. The runner reaches `applyHeal` ONLY (invariant spec); a failing probe ‚Üí `probe-unavailable`, heals nothing (a failed probe is never a corruption signal).
- **Evidence-assembler** (`dataIntegrityEvidenceAssembler`) ‚Äî folds the detect-producers' diagnoses ‚Üí per-collection classifier-input rows.
- **Interim actuator** (`DataRecoveryActuatorService`) ‚Äî `applyHeal` wraps the pure `dispatchHeal` safety core (rate-limit + anti-thrash). INTERIM: no heal operations wired ‚Üí every cleared action resolves to `deferred` (autonomous, recorded, **never a page**) ‚Äî strictly better than the deleted escalate, which paged into an operatorless void. The real ops extend the seam in #14134 / #14133 without touching the `applyHeal({action, collection, evidence, now})` contract (healOps + recentRunsReader + recordRun injected together, per the #14134 fail-closed invariant).
- **Orchestrator wiring** ‚Äî the actuator + the coverage-backed evidence-gatherer are injected into the runner; the `dataRecoveryActuatorService_` config-slot / `beforeSet` hook pairing holds the Orchestrator reactive-config invariant. Classification precision (`collectionSizes` / `documentsPresentByCollection`) wires with the ops in #14134 ‚Äî precision only changes the OUTCOME once a heal acts, and the interim all-defers, so coverage ‚Üí assemble ‚Üí classify ‚Üí defer is correct-by-construction.
- **Pipeline status-mapping** ‚Äî healed / clean ‚Üí completed.

## Out of scope ‚Äî sibling children of #14132

- Real heal OPERATIONS (re-embed / restore-delta-merge / defrag) ‚Äî #14134 (Grace).
- `quarantine`-from-serving safe-default op ‚Äî #14133.
- Re-route the 5 producers to raw-evidence ‚Äî #14138 (Ada).
- Orchestrator live-Chroma dimension gatherer + dimension-producer runner-seam ‚Äî #14130 / #14129.
- ADR-0025 / 0026 amendment, container-health producers, #14131 ‚Äî on #14132 directly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `escalateDiagnosis()` deleted from the data-integrity runner; the runner routes every actionable classification to `recoveryActuator.applyHeal` and nowhere else (invariant spec asserts no escalate/operator sink).
- [ ] The interim actuator resolves every action to a recorded outcome (`deferred` until ops wire), never an escalate/page status.
- [ ] The orchestrator injects the actuator + evidence-gatherer; the reactive-config slot/hook invariant holds.
- [ ] All affected specs green ‚Äî classifier, runner, assembler, actuator, orchestrator construction + invariants.

## Test Evidence

Affected specs (run green pre-PR): `dataIntegrityModeClassifier.spec`, `DataIntegrityDiagnosisService.spec` (incl. the no-escalate-sink invariant), `dataIntegrityEvidenceAssembler.spec`, `DataRecoveryActuatorService.spec`, `Orchestrator.spec` + `Orchestrator.invariants.spec` (the config slot/hook + fail-loud-read invariants).

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code). Origin session 1bb8a27b-ae0d-4668-a9a2-acbbe2387512.

The classifier DECIDES quarantine (correct); the actuator EXECUTES it (interim defers ‚Üí detects+records, contains nothing yet); containment lands with the wired containment op."

The escalate-deletion cutover's live wiring: the re-shaped runner consumes an evidence-gatherer (raw rows ‚Üí classifier) + the autonomous actuator's applyHeal sink, replacing the old coverage-gatherer + escalate-capable recoveryActuator injection.

- new dataRecoveryActuatorService_ config slot + beforeSet hook (interim: no ops injected ‚Üí all-defer, never a page; the wired-ops follow-up extends the seam)
- new dataIntegrityEvidenceGatherer getter: coverage producer ‚Üí assembler ‚Üí rows
- runner injection swapped: coverageGatherer‚ÜíevidenceGatherer, recoveryActuatorService‚ÜídataRecoveryActuatorService

Orchestrator construction + invariants specs green (config slot/hook + fail-loud-read invariants hold).

* docs(ai): correct interim all-defer accuracy ‚Äî quarantine routes-not-contains until the op is wired (#14183)

Grace's #14184 Approve+Follow-Up catch: the docs claimed the 'safe-default quarantine contain' as if the interim contains drift, but the interim actuator all-DEFERS (quarantine isn't wired ‚Üí it defers too). defer-all != contain. The classifier DECIDES quarantine (correct); the actuator EXECUTES it (interim defers ‚Üí detects+records, contains nothing yet); containment lands with the wired containment op.
